data.reasoning_trace: m001 hashrate_degradation warn/0.74. Hashrate agent: noise conf=0.88 — 30-min trajectory stationary at ~105.35 TH/s (+1.3% above nameplate), σ=0.38 TH/s, flat thermals (73.7–75.2°C), HSI 96–100, zero thermal throttle events. XGBoost fired on 1-min window variance (hr_std_1m=0.26, power_std_1m=13.8) that doesn't persist at 30-min resolution — textbook short-window false positive matching curated pattern xgboost_hashrate_fp_unanimous_noise. Miner is healthy. No fallback consultation warranted; primary verdict is decisive above 0.80. L1 observe, log for predictor calibration.

m002 hashrate_degradation crit/0.977. XGBoost fired on 1-min feature window (hr_mean_1m=87.59, hr_std_1m=0.37) with raw_score=0.988, predicting >20% drop within 30min. Hashrate agent: noise conf=0.89 — trajectory is stationary, hashrate flat at ~87.66 TH/s (~−0.38% vs nameplate 88.0, well inside ±2% eco-mode band), no step/ramp/sawtooth, temp stable 64–67°C, power steady ~2695W, HSI 93–100. Agent explicitly matches short_window_variance_false_positive and eco_mode_subnominal_offset_fp memory patterns — predictor misreading eco-mode persistent sub-nominal offset as early degradation. Single primary specialist returned decisive noise at conf=0.89 (>0.75 threshold) — no fallback needed. Also consistent with maestro memory pattern xgboost_hashrate_fp_unanimous_noise (noise ≥0.80, tight telemetry, miner running nominal). Downgrading to L1 observe despite crit severity. Log for predictor calibration. Will escalate if flag re-fires same miner next cycle or hashrate actually begins sloping.

m014 hashrate_degradation crit/0.97. Hashrate agent (primary): noise 0.91 — 30-min trajectory stationary at ~105.7 TH/s (+1.6% above nominal), σ≈0.44 TH/s (~0.4%), chip temps flat 74–76°C, hot_time_frac=0.0, HSI 94–100 with one transient dip. XGBoost tripped on hr_std_1m=0.44 in a 1-min window while the sustained 30-min curve shows nothing. Textbook short-window variance false positive — matches curated pattern xgboost_hashrate_fp_unanimous_noise. No fallback agent warranted; primary verdict is decisive at 0.91. Despite crit severity and high raw_score, miner is running above nominal with no real degradation signal. Downgrade to L1 observe. Log for predictor calibration. Re-escalate only if flag re-fires next cycle or realized hashrate begins to slope.

m016 hashrate_degradation crit/0.9783 (xgboost, p_drop_20pct_30min=0.9941). Hashrate agent: noise 0.88 — 30-min window is stationary at 86.75 TH/s ±0.43, no step/ramp/sawtooth; sub-nominal offset (-1.4%) consistent with known eco-mode de-rating. HSI 99.84, temps flat 65–67°C, ambient 22°C — no thermal co-oscillation. Model fired on hr_std_1m=0.43 and persistent eco-mode offset, classic short-window FP per documented patterns. Voltage fallback not warranted — primary returned decisive noise above 0.80. Combined read: XGBoost tripped on 1-min variance that does not persist at 30-min realized window. No real degradation signal. Matching `xgboost_hashrate_fp_unanimous_noise` pattern (decisive specialist noise ≥0.80 on crit hashrate flag). L1 observe; log for predictor calibration.

m045 hashrate_degradation crit/0.9793 (xgboost, p_drop=0.9976). Hashrate agent: noise conf=0.91 — 30-min realized trajectory is stationary at 104.82–106.18 TH/s (+1.4% above nominal), σ≈0.4 TH/s, HSI stable 92–99.87, chip temp steady 69.8–72.0°C, hot_time_frac=0.0. No step, ramp, or sawtooth. Matches `short_window_variance_false_positive` pattern: XGBoost fired on 1-min feature window variance that doesn't persist at 30-min scale. Voltage agent not dispatched — primary returned definitive noise above 0.80, no fallback warranted per routing rules. Also matches curated pattern `xgboost_hashrate_fp_unanimous_noise`: single decisive high-confidence noise verdict on an XGBoost crit flag. L1 observe; log for predictor calibration. No action taken.

m049 hashrate_degradation crit/0.9794 (XGBoost p_drop=0.9978). Hashrate specialist: real_signal conf=0.82, severity=warn — sawtooth 76.9–92.4 TH/s, σ≈3.3 and widening, chip_instability fault present on ~75% of 30-min records, HSI swings 75–99 in lockstep. Mean only −2.7% vs expected but variance is the story; temp co-oscillates as consequence of power-proportional output, not thermal causation (amb ~22°C, fans steady ~5800, no hot_time_frac). Specialist hint was human_review for hashboard inspection. Override rationale: a physical hashboard inspection is appropriate as *followup*, but it is not a time-critical action — the miner is degrading progressively, not failing now. Meanwhile, throttling to 80% for 60min is reversible, reduces per-chip stress on the unstable board, and buys time. Applying principle "least intrusive effective action + reversibility first": execute L3 throttle now AND raise an L2-style alert/ticket for operator to schedule hashboard inspection. Pure L4 queue with no mitigation leaves the unstable board running hot-cycling until a human reads the queue. Throttle floor respected (0.80 ≥ 0.70), duration within bounds. If variance continues widening post-throttle, next cycle escalates to L4 shutdown.

m040 hashrate_degradation warn/0.72. XGBoost fired at prob=0.54 (just above 0.50 warn threshold) on 1-min std elevation (hr_std_1m=3.41 vs hr_std_6m=3.04). Hashrate agent: noise 0.82 — 30-min window shows stationary hashrate ~111 TH/s (σ≈3.0), consistent with this S19j Pro's stable operating offset at ~96.5% nameplate. Symmetric variance, no slope/step/plateau shift. HSI oscillates in sync with natural variance. Temp co-moves proportionally with hash (work-proportional heat, not thermal throttling). Classic short-window variance false positive — predictor tripped on momentary std spike, 30-min sustained window flat within normal band. Single specialist returned decisive noise >0.80; no fallback consultation needed. Consistent with xgboost_hashrate_fp_unanimous_noise pattern. L1 observe.

m004 hashrate_degradation warn/0.75. Hashrate agent: noise conf=0.89 — 30-min trajectory stationary, hr oscillating 87.83–89.67 TH/s at +1% above nominal (expected 88.0 TH/s), z-score ~0 vs 6h baseline. HSI=99.86, hot_time_frac=0 throughout. XGBoost fired at prob=0.63, just above the 0.50 warn threshold, on 1-min window features; sustained 30-min window shows zero degradation. Pattern: short_window_variance false positive. Single specialist decisive noise verdict at 0.89 — no fallback needed. L1 observe, log for predictor calibration. No action warranted.

m019 hashrate_degradation warn/0.71 (xgboost raw_score 0.53, barely clears warn threshold). Hashrate agent: noise conf=0.82 — 30-min mean ≈115.3 TH/s sits at +0.3% above nominal (115.0), variance is symmetrical jitter (dips to ~107 immediately recovered to ~120.75) with no directional drift, no sawtooth thermal pattern. hr_std_1m=3.03 triggered the predictor on short-window variance; 30-min sustained window shows no degradation trajectory. HSI oscillating but driven by correlated thermal spikes at high-HR moments, not a degrading baseline. Classic short_window_variance_false_positive. Single primary specialist returned decisive noise at 0.82 — no fallback needed. Raw score barely above threshold, severity warn not crit, specialist verdict clear and well-reasoned. L1 observe; log for predictor calibration.

m008 hashrate_degradation warn/0.74. Hashrate agent (primary): noise conf=0.88 — 30-min hashrate stationary at ~89.0 TH/s ±0.39, actually +1.1% above nameplate. Chip temp flat 65–68°C, fans steady ~5780 RPM, HSI 86–99 with zero hot_time_frac. XGBoost fired on 1-min window variance (hr_std_1m=0.537) that doesn't persist at 30-min scale — textbook short-window false positive, consistent with curated pattern `xgboost_hashrate_fp_unanimous_noise`. No fallback needed; primary verdict is decisive at conf=0.88. No action warranted. L1 observe, log for predictor calibration.

m003 hashrate_degradation warn/0.70. Hashrate agent: noise conf=0.91 — miner running stationary at ~105.5 TH/s (±1.4% of 104.0 TH/s nameplate), no step/ramp/stall at any window, 1-min features consistent with 30-min baseline. XGBoost raw_score 0.506 barely clears the 0.5 threshold; agent explicitly IDs this as short-window variance false positive with hot_time_frac=0.0 and clean thermals. No voltage fallback needed — primary returned decisive noise above 0.80. Matches maestro_memory pattern xgboost_hashrate_fp_unanimous_noise (noise ≥0.80, miner running at or above nominal, flat thermals). L1 observe, log for predictor calibration. No action warranted.

m009 hashrate_degradation crit/0.96, XGBoost p_drop=0.928. Hashrate specialist: real_signal conf=0.88, crit — sustained wide-amplitude oscillation (71–109 TH/s, σ ~8–9 vs healthy ~3), mean ~97.5 TH/s (−6.2% vs nominal). Variance elevated at both 1m and 6m windows, so the curated xgboost_hashrate_fp_unanimous_noise pattern does NOT apply (and specialist ruled it out explicitly). Ambient flat 22.3–22.9°C, fans stable ~5800–6050 — chip temp co-moves with hashrate as symptom of compute instability, not thermal driver. Signature: chip_instability, miner-local. Specialist hinted alert_operator (L2), but single-specialist consultation on a high-confidence miner-local signal favors the reversible action over paging a human: throttle 80%/60min reduces chip thermal stress, may damp oscillation, is well above the 0.70 floor, and auto-expires. If re-flag fires post-throttle, next pass escalates to L4 for human review / maintenance scheduling. Confirming first-pass: throttle 80%/60min, L3.

m009 chip_instability_precursor crit/0.96. Hashrate agent: real_signal conf=0.93, crit — sub-minute 54→109 TH/s swings with tightly co-oscillating power (1709→3425W) and chip temp (51→75°C), ambient flat (22.5–23.1°C), fans steady ~5800–6000 RPM. Rules out measurement artifact and thermal throttling; signature is die-level instability (gate failure / voltage droop under load). HSI 98→96.7, variability 0.055→0.094 = actively escalating. Disagreement with first-pass: queueing L4 and waiting for human approval leaves the miner cycling 1.7kW→3.4kW at 5s cadence while we wait — that is precisely the condition worsening the fault. Reversibility-first says: drop to 70% hashrate floor now (L3, capped, reversible, within autonomy) to clamp the power/thermal excursions and buy time, AND page the operator in parallel for the durable decision (RMA / firmware / retire). Throttle does not preclude L4 follow-up; it de-risks the window before the human responds. L3 throttle 70%/30min + operator alert.

m033 hashrate_degradation crit/0.975. Hashrate agent: real_signal 0.96, crit — step-drop signature at ~08:03:41 UTC, ~76.9→~70.8 TH/s then flat plateau. Classic single-hashboard loss: power falls proportionally (2340→2170W), chip temp actually *dips* (59→56-57°C) ruling out thermal throttle, HSI monotonically declining 96.6→95.2, hr_std rising. Miner already ~27% below 104 TH/s nameplate pre-event → a prior board was likely already out; this is a *second* board loss on an already-degraded unit. No fallback needed (primary returned definitive real_signal >0.80). Action rationale: throttle is the wrong tool — degradation is hardware-mechanical, not thermal/power-loop; capping hashrate does not arrest further board failure and the unit is already under-nominal. Correct path is physical inspection/shutdown, which is L4 by policy (shutdown is in l4_always). Queuing for operator: decide shutdown-for-RMA vs isolate-and-schedule. No autonomous action — hardware loss pattern + third-board-imminent risk warrants human call. Confirming first-pass.

m013 hashrate_degradation warn/0.7076 (XGBoost p=0.519). Hashrate specialist: real_signal conf=0.93, severity=crit — sustained flat plateau ~67.7 TH/s vs 104.0 nameplate (-34.9%), ratio 0.651 matches textbook 2-of-3 hashboard survival (0.667). Proportional power drop (~2175W vs ~3250W) confirms silicon loss, not measurement artifact. Thermals flat (56°C), fans nominal, TE=0.0 → miner running unprofitably. Predictor underestimated: actual degradation far exceeds its -20% threshold. Synthesis: confirmed hardware step (hashboard failure), not noise. Throttle is inapplicable — it neither recovers revenue nor protects a miner whose silicon is already gone; reversibility logic doesn't help here. Decision requires physical inspection + likely board replacement, and the shutdown-vs-run-degraded call is consequential. Alert operator with crit recommendation; shutdown queued for human approval per L4 policy. Confirming first-pass.

m040 chip_instability_precursor warn/0.59. Hashrate agent: real_signal 0.78 — 30-min hashrate oscillates 94–120 TH/s (σ≈6.2 TH/s vs healthy ≤2 TH/s), mean ~107 TH/s (−7% vs nameplate). Temp co-moves with hashrate/power rather than leading, ruling out simple thermal sawtooth; pattern consistent with intermittent hash-chip dropout/recovery. HSI variability component rising monotonically (0.031→0.036), sigma_hash climbing 3.42→3.98 — variance trending up, not stationary. No fallback agent consulted; hashrate agent returned definitive real_signal. Flag confidence is moderate (0.59), specialist confidence is meaningful (0.78) but below the 0.80 threshold that would support L3 autonomous throttle on its own. Recommended action from specialist is alert_operator. Reversibility principle: chip instability precursor without confirmed step-drop does not yet justify autonomous throttle — progression is possible but not confirmed. Routing L2: alert operator to watch for escalation to sustained low plateau or step-drop, at which point throttle L3 or maintenance L4 would be warranted.

m033 chip_instability_precursor warn/0.61. Hashrate agent: real_signal conf=0.97, severity=crit. Assessment: hashrate locked at ~71 TH/s vs 104 TH/s expected (−31.7%), sustained flat plateau for 30+ min, no thermal co-oscillation (chip temp stationary 56–58°C, ambient 23°C). Power draw also reduced ~2175W vs ~3300W nominal — pattern consistent with complete hashboard loss (1-of-3 boards down, ~67% remaining ≈ observed 68%). Step-drop with flat plateau is textbook hashboard_failure, not short-window noise. Fault_injected field confirms hashboard_failure. Specialist recommends alert_operator. Synthesis: single specialist returned decisive real_signal at 0.97 with crit severity estimate — this is not ambiguous. The underlying fault (hashboard failure) is a hardware event requiring physical inspection or replacement; this is not a reversible L3 throttle candidate. Throttling a miner already running at 68% capacity due to a dead board provides no benefit. The appropriate action is L2 alert to operator with recommendation to inspect/replace hashboard. L4 (human_review/shutdown) is defensible but L2 is correct first step — operator should assess whether to keep running on 2 boards or pull the unit. Not queuing L4 shutdown as miner is stable thermally and degraded-but-functional operation may be acceptable pending maintenance scheduling.

m041 hashrate_degradation warn/0.72. Hashrate agent: noise conf=0.88 — 30-min trajectory stationary at ~104.5 TH/s (σ≈0.4), range ±1.7% of nominal, no step/ramp/sawtooth. XGBoost fired on short 1-min variance (hr_std_1m=0.277) — classic short-window false positive per curated memory pattern. Specialist flags hot_time_frac=1.0 and chip temps 87–91°C as noteworthy for thermal tracking, but hashrate itself shows zero degradation signal. Single primary specialist returned decisive noise above 0.80 — no fallback warranted. Thermal note logged for environment_agent context if a thermal flag follows, but no hashrate action indicated. L1 observe.

m013 chip_instability_precursor warn/0.52 (XGBoost fired on hashrate_th_30m_std=9.67). Hashrate specialist: real_signal conf=0.95, severity crit — sustained flat plateau at ~67.8 TH/s vs nominal 104.0 TH/s (−34.6% step drop), power proportionally reduced (~2150–2230W vs ~3250W), chip temps cool and stable (55–58°C), ambient 23°C. Shape is textbook hashboard failure, not thermal throttling or precursor instability. The XGBoost variance signal is residual noise on surviving boards; the real event is the step drop that already occurred. Specialist conf 0.95 overrides the borderline flag confidence. This is a confirmed hardware state change, not a precursor — throttle is not a valid remedy (cannot restore an offline board) and retire/maintenance decisions sit in the L4 bucket by policy. Alerting operator with full context for maintenance scheduling or RMA decision. No autonomous action.

m019 chip_instability_precursor warn/0.541. Hashrate agent: real_signal conf=0.88, severity upgraded to crit. Pattern is chaotic multi-chip dropout/recovery — hashrate swinging 50–119 TH/s against 115 TH/s nominal, 30m mean ~87.5 TH/s (−24% nameplate), power locked in perfect correlation with hashrate (1557–3651 W), ruling out supply-side jitter. Chip temps co-oscillate with hashrate (not thermal throttle governor). Ambient stable at 23°C, fans steady — not environmental. Specialist explicitly distinguishes this from short-window variance FP: trajectory is emphatically non-stationary, HSI oscillating 65.8–93.9 with persistently elevated variability component. This is active chip instability, not a predictor artifact. Flag confidence was 0.541 but specialist confidence is 0.88 on real_signal crit — specialist telemetry review is decisive. Specialist recommended alert_operator. Autonomy: chip instability at this severity (−24% sustained, chaotic oscillation) warrants operator review before any autonomous throttle — degradation may be accelerating and the appropriate response (throttle depth, maintenance scheduling, potential hashboard swap) requires human judgment. L2 alert with recommendation to throttle or schedule maintenance inspection.

action_params.reason: Sustained power_sag fault tag causing ~13% hashrate derate (90.5 vs 104.0 TH/s). Power proportionally reduced. Thermal stable. Requires power-side root cause investigation — PSU or site supply to m043.
action_params.suggested_next_step: Inspect m043 PSU and upstream power feed; consult power_agent for root cause confirmation.

data.reasoning_trace: m043 hashrate_degradation warn/0.72. Hashrate agent: real_signal 0.82 — miner locked in stationary plateau ~90.5 TH/s vs 104.0 TH/s nameplate (~−13%), ultra-low within-window σ (0.38 TH/s), power proportionally reduced (~2880W vs ~3472W expected), thermal stable (69–71°C, no co-variation). `power_sag` fault tag on every record — operative signal is a sustained power sag holding the miner at a derated operating point. Not a ramp, not chip/hashboard failure, not a thermal driver. Step-down already occurred before the observation window; condition is stable-but-degraded. Fallback voltage_agent not invoked — hashrate agent verdict is definitive (real_signal, conf 0.82 > inconclusive threshold). No memory match. Per agent recommendation and flow 1 guidance: 1/1 real_signal but root cause is power-side, not miner-local compute fault — autonomous throttle would not address a supply-side sag and could mask the signal. Alert operator for PSU/supply investigation on m043. L2.

action_params.suggested_followup: dispatch power_agent and voltage_agent for PSU/supply diagnosis; consider scheduled maintenance if sag recurs

data.reasoning_trace: m031 hashrate_degradation warn/0.73. Hashrate agent: real_signal 0.78 — sustained ramp-down 93.7→91.7 TH/s over 30-min (-10.7% vs 104 TH/s baseline), power co-descends 2930→2860W, HSI 99.08-99.10, zero thermal stress. Signature is textbook voltage/power sag, miner-local. Overriding first-pass L3 throttle: (1) specialist explicitly recommended alert_operator and flagged that power_agent/voltage_agent should own the causal investigation — we did not consult the fallback, so we lack confirmation of whether the sag is PSU-internal (throttle helps) vs upstream supply (throttle is cosmetic, masks the real issue). (2) Only one specialist consulted; acting L3 on a single-specialist read when that specialist itself hinted at operator routing violates listen-to-the-specialists. (3) Throttling without diagnosis reduces symptom visibility and could delay root-cause identification on a confirmed fault. Emit L2 alert with full trace; operator decides between immediate throttle and dispatching power/voltage diagnosis. Reversible path preserved, no autonomy inflation.

m041 fan_anomaly warn/0.88 (rule_engine, fan idx 2 @ 571 RPM vs 3000 threshold). Environment agent: real_signal conf=0.82 — localized hardware failure: zone peers m010/m011/m012 healthy at 5900–6500 RPM, site ambient stable 23.2–23.8°C, but m041 chip temps 87–91°C with degraded cooling. No fallback per routing (fan failures unambiguous). Specialist hinted alert_operator, but chip temps already in thermal-risk band and 3-of-4 fan operation cannot sustain nominal load — waiting for human approval before protective action is the wrong tradeoff. Throttle to 70%/60min is reversible, capped, rate-limited — squarely within L3 envelope. This reduces thermal load immediately; fan replacement itself remains a maintenance action for the operator (outside my scope), and the flag+trace surface that need. Confirming Sonnet first-pass.

m043 chip_instability_precursor warn/0.70. Hashrate agent: real_signal 0.82 — sustained flat plateau at ~90.4 TH/s vs 104 TH/s nameplate (~−13% deficit), step-drop shape thermally decoupled (chip temps 69–72°C, fans nominal ~5800 RPM). Within-window σ only ~0.37 TH/s; XGBoost std features likely captured the step transition into plateau. Assessment: hardware-side reduction, likely one hashboard derated or offline. Telemetry annotation notes `fault_injected: power_sag` — power/voltage causation not yet ruled out; fallback voltage_agent consultation warranted but hashrate agent explicitly flagged this cross-check need. No voltage agent response in hand. Combined read: −13% sustained deficit approaching crit threshold, hashboard loss the leading hypothesis, but power/voltage causation unresolved. Autonomous throttle not appropriate here — this may be a hardware fault requiring physical inspection, not a thermal margin issue a throttle would address. Alerting operator with full context and recommendation to run voltage_agent cross-check before deciding on maintenance action. L2.

m010 hashrate_degradation warn/0.7152. XGBoost raw score 0.538 — barely above 0.50 warn threshold. Hashrate agent (primary): noise conf=0.88, severity=info. Miner running at 105.9 TH/s mean vs 104.0 nameplate (+2%), σ=0.37 TH/s (0.35%), stationary over 30-min window with no step/ramp/stall. Chip temps 87–90°C with no monotonic trend, no hashrate co-oscillation indicating thermal throttle. Agent explicitly matches short_window_variance_false_positive pattern — model fired on 1-min variance, not sustained degradation. Maestro memory pattern xgboost_hashrate_fp_unanimous_noise applies: primary specialist called noise ≥0.80, miner telemetry nominal. Downgrade to L1 observe. No autonomous action warranted.

m029 fan_anomaly warn/0.88 (rule_engine, fan_idx 2 @ 1093 RPM vs 3000 threshold, peers 6400+). Environment agent: real_signal conf=0.82 — miner-local hardware failure, ambient 23.5°C stable, chip at 88°C vs zone peers ~75°C at similar power, confirming lost fan duty. Specialist hint was alert_operator; I'm upgrading the response envelope, not the autonomy level: active thermal risk (88°C, one fan dead) warrants immediate protective throttle to 70%/30min — reversible, rate-limited, within L3 bounds — while the operator is paged for fan_idx 2 replacement. Throttle buys thermal headroom now; observe-only would let the chip climb further. Not shutdown-warranting (single fan, no runaway slope). Co-occurring m010 pattern noted but scoped separately. Confirming Sonnet first-pass. L3.

action_params.recommendation: Immediate shutdown of m040 to prevent cascading hashboard damage; throttle is contraindicated (not thermal/load-driven).

data.reasoning_trace: m040 chip_instability_precursor warn/0.73 (xgboost_rolling_variance, top feature hashrate_th_30m_std=10.52). Hashrate specialist: real_signal conf=0.91, severity upgraded warn→crit. Bilateral oscillation 43.9–116.2 TH/s vs 115 TH/s nameplate, 30m mean ~84 TH/s (−26.9%); chip temp and power co-oscillate in lockstep with hashrate (43.9 TH/s@46°C ↔ 92.7 TH/s@65°C), ruling out thermal sawtooth or pool stall — this is chip substrate intermittent dropout. HSI collapsed from ~92 to 69–74; sigma_hash ~17 TH/s is ~10x normal envelope. Unambiguous verdict, no fallback warranted. Throttle contraindicated: reducing target hashrate won't arrest intermittent substrate failure and may mask progression. Shutdown is the correct protective action but is L4-mandatory per autonomy ladder (never auto-shutdown). Queuing for human approval with high-urgency alert; operator should expect possible hashboard retirement. Confirming first-pass Sonnet decision.

m012 hashrate_degradation crit/0.942 (xgboost, p_drop_20pct_30min=0.864). Hashrate agent: noise conf=0.91 — 30-min trajectory stationary at ~101.1 TH/s ±0.47, no step/ramp/sawtooth, HSI ≥99.24, thermal_stress=0.0, temps stable 71–74°C. XGBoost features (hr_mean_1m=105.1 vs actual 30-min ~101.1) appear to have captured a DVFS/mode transition artifact, not real degradation. The ~12% offset below nameplate is explained by a pre-existing power_sag condition — stable, non-degrading plateau. Matches known pattern `xgboost_hashrate_fp_unanimous_noise`: single specialist returns noise ≥0.80 with no real telemetry signal. Despite crit severity and high model confidence, the specialist verdict is decisive — escalating to L2 here would be alert fatigue. Downgrade to L1 observe; flag for predictor calibration review if re-fires on m012 within next cycle.
